column91:
The Rolex Yacht-Master II reference 116680 is a bold and highly technical Rolex sports watch designed specifically for competitive sailing. Crafted entirely from Oystersteel, this 44 mm timepiece stands out with its white dial and striking blue Cerachrom ceramic bezel, giving it one of the most recognizable looks in the Rolex lineup. ￼

At the heart of the watch is Rolex’s Calibre 4161 automatic movement, featuring an innovative programmable regatta countdown timer with mechanical memory that can be synchronized on the fly—an extremely rare complication in mechanical watches. This function allows sailors to precisely time the crucial starting sequence of a yacht race. ￼

The watch uses Rolex’s patented Ring Command bezel, which interacts directly with the movement to set and control the countdown function. Built with a 44 mm Oyster case, sapphire crystal, and a screw-down Triplock crown, it is waterproof to 100 meters and delivers about 72 hours of power reserve. ￼

First introduced in 2013 as the first stainless-steel Yacht-Master II, the 116680 became one of Rolex’s most distinctive professional models before the Yacht-Master II line was discontinued in 2024, making it increasingly collectible. ￼

Rolex Cosmograph Daytona – 126500LN – White Dial “Panda” – 2024 – Mint Condition – Complete Set

The Rolex Daytona 126500LN with white dial, widely known as the “Panda,” is the newest generation of Rolex’s most iconic chronograph. Introduced as the successor to the 116500LN, this updated reference features subtle but meaningful refinements to the case, dial, and bezel design.

The crisp white dial with contrasting black subdials delivers the classic Panda aesthetic that has become one of the most collectible modern Rolex configurations. The refined black Cerachrom bezel with updated metal ring detail enhances both durability and visual depth.

Crafted in Oystersteel, the watch is powered by Rolex’s in-house Caliber 4131, offering improved efficiency, a 72-hour power reserve, and Superlative Chronometer precision.

ROLEX Day-Date 40 Olive Dial Everose Gold President 228235

The Rolex Day-Date 40 reference 228235 is one of the most iconic executions of the modern Day-Date line, featuring the highly sought-after Olive Dial configuration. Produced in 2019, this example pairs the rich sunburst olive green dial with applied Everose gold hour markers and the signature fluted bezel. The 40mm 18k Everose gold case is fitted on the classic President bracelet with concealed Crownclasp, maintaining the model’s unmistakable presence and refined proportions.

The Olive Dial variant has become one of the most desirable configurations within the contemporary Day-Date collection. Its warm tone complements the Everose gold case exceptionally well, creating strong collector demand and long-term appeal. As a modern flagship reference, the 228235 continues to hold a prominent position in the Rolex lineup.

column241:
The Rolex Datejust Turn-O-Graph Ref.116264 is a distinctive and highly collectible variation of the classic Datejust, blending Rolex’s timeless elegance with subtle sport-inspired design elements. Often nicknamed the “Thunderbird,” this reference stands out with its fluted rotating bezel, striking black dial, and signature red accents — including the red seconds hand and red date numerals — giving the watch a unique and dynamic character within the Datejust lineup.

Crafted in stainless steel with a white gold bezel, the 36mm case offers the perfect balance between sport and dress versatility, making it equally suited for daily wear or refined occasions. Powered by Rolex’s reliable automatic movement and featuring the iconic Cyclops date display, the Turn-O-Graph delivers both functionality and enduring style.

This example is offered as a complete full set, including original box and papers, making it especially appealing for collectors and enthusiasts seeking a well-preserved and complete package.

column275:
The Rolex Day-Date 18238 from 1999 is a timeless symbol of prestige, crafted in 18k yellow gold and featuring the iconic President bracelet. However, this particular example stands out as a truly unique collector’s piece, likely gifted as part of Phil Nevin’s signing bonus during his time with the San Diego Padres.

What makes this watch especially rare is its custom dial, stamped with the Padres logo, a striking personalization that blends luxury with baseball history. The champagne dial—a classic hallmark of the Day-Date—offers an elegant contrast to the bold team emblem. Inside, the watch houses the Caliber 3155 movement, ensuring precision timekeeping with day and date functionality.

Adding to its significance, the caseback is engraved with Phil Nevin’s name, marking it as a likely commemorative gift celebrating his arrival in San Diego. Rolex watches customized for athletes and executives are seldom seen on the open market, making this a one-of-a-kind timepiece for both Rolex collectors and Padres fans alike.

Phil Nevin

column347:
The Rolex Datejust 36 Ref. 126234 Wimbledon is one of the most popular modern versions of the Datejust line. It combines classic Rolex design with the distinctive “Wimbledon” slate dial that has green Roman numerals inspired by the famous tennis tournament.

Description

The Rolex Datejust 126234 Wimbledon features a 36 mm Oystersteel case paired with a fluted bezel made from 18 kt white gold, giving the watch a refined yet sporty appearance. The standout feature is the sunray slate-grey “Wimbledon” dial with black Roman numerals outlined in green, along with a unique luminous marker at 9 o’clock. The dial includes a date window at 3 o’clock with Rolex’s Cyclops magnifier, making the date easy to read. ￼

Inside the watch is Rolex’s Caliber 3235 automatic movement, a high-precision “Superlative Chronometer” movement that offers approximately 70 hours of power reserve and excellent resistance to shocks and magnetism. ￼

The watch is typically paired with either the Jubilee bracelet or Oyster bracelet, both equipped with the Easylink comfort extension system for easy size adjustment. It is also water-resistant up to 100 meters (330 ft) thanks to the sealed Oyster case and screw-down crown. ￼

Complete with the factory Rolex box and papers, this is a well-documented Daytona from a highly desirable production era.

column424:
The Explorer II 16570 holds a unique place in the Rolex sport lineup since its 40mm case and fixed 24-hour steel bezel give it real tool watch character, distinguishing it from the more flashy sport models. Produced from 1989 to 2011, this reference spans over two decades and offers a blend of vintage charm with reliable modern build making it a hit for collectors. With its understated black dial, bold GMT hand, and steel bezel, this watch hits that sweet spot where durability meets daily wearability while remaining refreshingly subtle.

This reference features a 40mm Oystersteel case, fixed stainless steel 24-hour bezel, black dial with luminous hour markers, and independent hour hand/24-hour hand functionality for dual-time tracking. It’s powered by Rolex’s Caliber 3185 automatic movement, offering dependable chronometer level performance and excellent serviceability. The watch is finished on a stainless steel Oyster bracelet with solid links and a secure clasp.

This reference features a 40mm black dial with Chromalight lume, a bidirectional rotating Cerachrom bezel with engraved numerals, and Rolex’s trusted dual-time functionality for tracking an additional time zone. Powered by the Rolex Caliber 3186 automatic movement, offering a 50-hour power reserve. The watch is fitted with the Oyster bracelet and secured by the Oysterlock clasp.

column532:
Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 – Blue Dial – Ref. 124300 – 2024

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 Ref. 124300 is the purest expression of Rolex design — clean, balanced, and instantly recognizable. Featuring the vibrant sunray-finished blue dial, this 2024 model captures the essence of understated luxury while offering modern proportions and everyday versatility.

Crafted from Rolex’s proprietary Oystersteel, the 41 mm case delivers durability, corrosion resistance, and a refined polish that reflects light with precision. The luminous Chromalight hour markers and hands provide exceptional legibility, while the smooth bezel preserves the watch’s minimalist aesthetic.

Powered by the in-house Caliber 3230, this timepiece offers a 70-hour power reserve, Superlative Chronometer precision, and Rolex’s patented Chronergy escapement for enhanced efficiency and reliability. Whether worn casually or formally, the Oyster Perpetual 41 remains one of the most timeless and investment-worthy references in modern Rolex production.

For collectors and first-time Rolex buyers alike, the 124300 represents entry into one of the most iconic watch lineages in history — simple, pure, and enduring.

column540:
Rolex GMT-Master II 'Pepsi' - 126710BLRO

Nicknamed the “Pepsi” for its distinctive red-and-blue bezel, the Rolex GMT-Master II Ref. 126710BLRO traces its roots back to 1955 when Rolex introduced the original GMT-Master for Pan Am pilots who needed to track multiple time zones during long-haul flights. The red and blue bezel quickly became the visual signature of the model—red for daylight hours and blue for nighttime on the 24-hour scale.

Today’s GMT-Master II continues that legacy with Rolex’s modern Cerachrom ceramic bezel and the in-house Calibre 3285 movement, offering first class engineering with one of the most recognizable designs in watchmaking. Decades of aviation heritage, GMT functionality, and an instantly recognizable bezel have cemented the Pepsi GMT as one of the true icons of the watch world.

Comes as a complete set

column566:
This is an authentic Rolex Explorer ref. 214270 (Mark II), a highly regarded 39MM modern Explorer that perfectly balances Rolex’s tool-watch heritage with refined everyday wearability. The Mark II update improved dial proportions and legibility, making this reference one of the most desirable Explorer configurations.

This 2010 example is in Mint Condition and is part of the Rolex Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) program. It remains under Rolex factory warranty, offering exceptional peace of mind.

About the Rolex Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) Program
Rolex Certified Pre-Owned watches are authenticated, inspected, and certified directly by Rolex. As part of the CPO process, the watch undergoes a comprehensive evaluation, and is fully serviced by Rolex if required to meet Rolex’s strict technical and cosmetic standards. The watch is then tested for accuracy and water resistance and issued an official Rolex CPO warranty, ensuring factory-level performance and reliability.

Only watches that meet Rolex’s exacting criteria are approved and released under the CPO program.

Complete with the factory Rolex box and papers, this Explorer offers manufacturer-backed confidence along with timeless design.

column572:
ROLEX Yacht-Master II White Dial Steel Oyster Bracelet 116680

The ROLEX Yacht-Master II reference 116680 is a regatta chronograph designed specifically for competitive sailing. Produced in 2015, this now discontinued model features a white dial with bold blue accents and a programmable countdown function controlled by the distinctive blue Cerachrom Ring Command bezel. The 44mm stainless steel case is paired with the iconic Oyster bracelet, creating a strong yet refined presence on the wrist.

As one of Rolex’s most technically advanced sports models, the Yacht-Master II remains highly sought after for its unique regatta complication and bold design. With its discontinued status and desirable steel configuration, reference 116680 continues to hold strong appeal among collectors and enthusiasts seeking a distinctive Rolex sports watch.

Dive into excellence with the 2025 Rolex Sea-Dweller, a timepiece engineered for those who demand precision, resilience, and timeless luxury. Crafted from Oystersteel and built to withstand depths of 4,000 feet (1,220 meters), this watch is the ultimate tool for professional divers and connoisseurs alike.

Featuring a 43mm case, Cerachrom bezel insert in black ceramic, and a crystal-clear Chromalight display, the Sea-Dweller offers unmatched readability in the darkest conditions. At its heart beats the Caliber 3235 movement, delivering Rolex’s cutting-edge performance with a 70-hour power reserve.

This 2025 edition boasts subtle refinements in case shape and bracelet comfort, while preserving its iconic helium escape valve and cyclops-free design — a nod to its uncompromising heritage.

Whether you’re charting the ocean’s depths or navigating the heights of sophistication, the Sea-Dweller makes a bold statement of strength and style.

This superb chronometer features an Bright black dial, which is the perfect backdrop for the luminous 18k Yellow Gold index hour markers and hands, making it easy to read the time even in low light conditions. column769:
The Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41, reference 134300-0006, introduced in 2025, embodies the latest generation of one of Rolex’s most iconic timepieces. As the direct successor to the 124300, this model refines the essence of the Oyster Perpetual line with subtle updates in design, ergonomics, and finishing, while preserving its reputation as a timeless, versatile watch that appeals to collectors and enthusiasts alike.

The 41mm case, crafted in corrosion-resistant Oystersteel, offers a perfect balance of presence and comfort on the wrist. Inside beats the in-house Caliber 3230, equipped with Rolex’s Chronergy escapement, Parachrom hairspring, and a robust 70-hour power reserve — combining precision, reliability, and efficiency. The smooth, polished bezel frames the striking pistachio green dial, whose refined sunray finish and luminous Chromalight markers ensure clarity and elegance in every light. Completing the look is the classic Oyster bracelet, with its three-piece solid links and Oysterclasp, ensuring durability and comfort throughout daily wear.

column843:
One of the most classic small-size Datejusts from Rolex’s solid-gold era, the 69178 is the reference a lot of people picture when they think of a true vintage ladies Rolex. The all-yellow-gold build on a Jubilee bracelet has a look and weight that two-tone never really delivers. If you want a real gold Datejust in the original 26mm proportions, this is the kind of piece that doesn’t need an introduction.

This reference features a 26mm 18k yellow gold case with a fluted yellow gold bezel and a Datejust dial with applied hour markers and a date at 3 under a Cyclops. It’s powered by Rolex’s automatic in-house caliber from the 69xxx series, known for its straightforward serviceability and quickset date on later production. The watch is fitted with an 18k yellow gold Jubilee bracelet and secured by a Rolex folding clasp.

column853:
✅ What is the 126233 with Motif Dial
• The 126233 belongs to the Rolex Datejust 36 family — a 36 mm model (size varies slightly depending on bracelet and finishing) mixing classic proportions with modern Rolex engineering. ￼
• “Motif dial” refers to special textured/patterned dials introduced by Rolex on some Datejust 36 models — instead of the usual smooth or sunburst dial surface, the dial features a decorative pattern or motif (e.g. a fluted‑bezel‑inspired motif or a more expressive “palm motif”) that gives the dial depth and visual interest. ￼
• The 126233 watch is crafted in “Yellow Rolesor” — meaning the case is stainless steel (Oystersteel) with components (like the bezel) in 18 kt yellow gold, giving a two‑tone steel-and-gold finish. ￼

🛠️ Build, Materials & Design Features

column854:
The ultimate tool watch built for true exploration. Designed for speleologists and potholers, the Explorer II helped them keep track of time via the large luminous 24-hour hand, enabling adventurers to distinguish day from night in environments where the sun never penetrates. The Explorer II with White Dial is the most desirable variation of the Rolex Explorer line, earning its "Polar" nickname from its clean, high-contrast aesthetic.

This watch features a 42mm stainless steel case with sapphire crystal and white dial. The Explorer II features a date display, an additional orange 24-hour hand and a fixed bezel with 24-hour graduations, enabling day to be distinguished from night. Powered by Rolex Calibre 3187 movement and fitted with a matching stainless steel Oyster bracelet secured by an Oysterclasp.

column927:
The Rolex Datejust is one of the most significant models in the history of watchmaking. First introduced in 1945, it was the first self-winding wristwatch to display the date on the dial. The Datejust quickly became a symbol of prestige and luxury, and has been worn by some of the world's most influential figures, including US President Eisenhower and Martin Luther King Jr. Over the years, Rolex has continued to refine the design of the Datejust, adding new features and innovations while staying true to its classic look and feel. Today, the Datejust remains one of the most popular and recognizable watches in the world, a testament to its timeless design and enduring appeal.
This particular 937X serial example, circa 1987 falls in the 5 digit reference era which benefits from the addition of a quickset date function. It boasts a beautiful "linen" dial with warm tritium patina matching its hands. Overall, this piece has remained in excellent vintage condition with a thick case displaying sharp edges and sharp bezel. The bracelet has medium but frankly feels silky and comfortable as a result and will fit an approximate 7 inch wrist. A steel/white gold fluted Datejust with Jubilee bracelet is arguably the most classic and dynamic iteration of the model and a great option for just about anyone.

column933:
The Rolex Explorer was first introduced in 1953 and is notable for its association with adventure. The design emphasizes durability, reliability, and legibility in extreme conditions. It features a beautifully simple and balanced dial, luminous hour markers, and a robust stainless steel case. The watch is also equipped with Rolex's patented Oyster case technology, which provides exceptional water resistance and protection from dust and other environmental hazards. The Explorer has been worn on notable expeditions and adventures and has become a symbol of Rolex's commitment to quality and reliability in extreme conditions, and has earned a top spot in the list of Rolex's iconic models.
This modern ref. 124270 is the evolution of the ref. 114270 which features some key updates including a new cal. 3230 with 70-hour power reserved and added shock resistance, reformulated blue Chromalight luminous material which glows longer and brighter, as well as more modern and solid braclet with EasyLink extension system. It remains one of the cleanest and most practical sport watches in the model line that's truly fit for any occasion. This example comes as a complete set from new with warranty papers from '23. It remains in unpolished condition with light wear and is ready to enjoy for many years to come.

The ROLEX Sky-Dweller reference 336239 is a contemporary travel-focused timepiece, featuring a black dial with luminous hour markers and the signature annual calendar display. The 18k white gold case is paired with a fluted white gold bezel and mounted on a black Oysterflex strap with Oysterclasp. The 42mm case offers strong wrist presence while maintaining refined proportions suitable for both formal and sport settings.

As one of ROLEX’s most technically advanced models, the Sky-Dweller continues to see strong demand among collectors and enthusiasts. The white gold configuration with black dial remains one of the most desirable modern combinations, offering understated luxury with everyday versatility. Its annual calendar and dual time functionality further enhance its appeal in today’s market.

column973:
🌊 Rolex Sea-Dweller 126603 – 43mm Steel & Yellow Gold (Complete Set, Mint Condition)

Bold. Capable. Uncompromising.

The Rolex Sea-Dweller 126603 is where professional-grade functionality meets elevated luxury. Crafted in Oystersteel and 18k yellow gold, this 43mm timepiece delivers the power and technical prowess of a deep-sea instrument with the visual presence and refinement expected of a modern Rolex.

This mint condition, complete set is an exceptional opportunity to own one of Rolex’s most versatile and impressive two-tone sports models.

✨ Design & Aesthetic Presence

Striking Two-Tone Rolesor Finish
The watch balances rugged durability with luxurious refinement. The combination of brushed Oystersteel and polished 18k yellow gold offers both robustness and prestige — equally at home under a diving suit or a tailored cuff.

Gloss Black Dial with Chromalight Lume
The high-gloss black dial is accented by gold-trimmed hour markers and hands filled with long-lasting blue Chromalight, ensuring maximum legibility in all conditions — and unmistakable elegance in any setting.

Cerachrom Ceramic Bezel
The unidirectional rotatable bezel, crafted in virtually scratchproof black Cerachrom, features gold-filled numerals and graduations. It is highly resistant to fading and enhances the timepiece’s striking contrast and visual depth.

💪 Performance & Engineering Excellence

43mm Oyster Case
The Sea-Dweller’s larger case size offers significant wrist presence without compromising balance or wearability. Its architecture ensures a water resistance of up to 1,220 meters (4,000 feet), thanks to its patented helium escape valve and Triplock crown system.

Rolex Caliber 3235 Movement
Inside, the watch is powered by the in-house Caliber 3235 — a COSC-certified, Superlative Chronometer automatic movement with a 70-hour power reserve, anti-magnetic properties, and outstanding long-term reliability.

Oyster Bracelet with Glidelock Extension
The two-tone Oyster bracelet offers a seamless blend of comfort and sophistication. Rolex’s Glidelock and Fliplock extension systems ensure an adjustable, secure fit — whether over a wetsuit or during daily wear.

📦 Complete Set – Collector Ready

column1030:
Introducing the unworn 2025 Rolex Oyster Perpetual 36 Ref. 126000-0011, an elegant and timeless creation that combines refined aesthetics with Rolex’s renowned precision. Crafted from Oystersteel, the 36mm case delivers both durability and understated sophistication, enhanced by a smooth polished bezel that radiates discreet brilliance. The captivating Pistachio-green dial, distinctive within the Oyster Perpetual collection, adds a modern twist to this classic timepiece. Its minimalist layout features luminous silver-tone hands and baton hour markers, with double markers at 3, 6, and 9 o’clock for balance and enhanced readability. Each hour marker is echoed by a bold black square integrated into the minute track around the dial, adding contemporary flair. A scratch-resistant sapphire crystal and solid screw-down case back protect the display, while the screw-down crown with Rolex’s Twinlock double waterproofness system ensures security in all environments. At the heart of this model beats the Rolex Calibre 3230, an advanced self-winding movement with 31 jewels, a power reserve of approximately 70 hours, and stop-seconds functionality for precise time setting. Certified as a Superlative Chronometer, it guarantees exceptional accuracy and reliability. Completing the design is the classic Oyster bracelet in Oystersteel with three-piece solid links, offering both strength and comfort, secured by the folding Oysterclasp. Water-resistant to 100m, the Oyster Perpetual 36 embodies Rolex’s enduring philosophy of elegance, functionality, and timeless style.

column1033:
The Rolex Day-Date 40 reference 228235-0053 is a masterclass in modern luxury. Crafted from solid 18 kt Everose gold and paired with a striking chocolate dial featuring elegant stick hour markers, this configuration is both understated and undeniably refined. With its iconic fluted bezel and President bracelet, this Day-Date remains one of the most distinguished symbols of status and craftsmanship in the Rolex lineup.

The 40mm Oyster case is milled from a single block of Everose gold, offering water resistance to 100 meters. At its heart lies Rolex’s caliber 3255 — a certified Superlative Chronometer movement with a 70-hour power reserve and advanced Chronergy escapement for outstanding accuracy and reliability. The chocolate sunray dial is clean and balanced, enhanced by baton-style markers and the signature day and date apertures at 12 and 3 o'clock.

Completing the look is the iconic President bracelet, known for its semi-circular three-piece links and concealed folding Crownclasp — combining elegance with a secure fit.

column1034:
Rolex GMT-Master II “Batgirl” – 126710BLNR (2024)

The Rolex GMT-Master II “Batgirl” reference 126710BLNR is one of the most sought-after modern Rolex sports watches, seamlessly blending professional functionality with contemporary elegance. Instantly recognizable by its iconic black and blue Cerachrom bezel and refined Jubilee bracelet, the Batgirl has become a cornerstone for collectors and investors alike.

Crafted from Oystersteel, Rolex’s proprietary stainless steel alloy, this GMT-Master II offers exceptional resistance to corrosion while maintaining a brilliant finish. The 40mm Oyster case is paired with a deep black dial featuring luminous Chromalight hour markers, ensuring optimal legibility in all conditions. Designed originally for international pilots, this model allows the wearer to track multiple time zones simultaneously, making it both highly functional and unmistakably prestigious.

Powering the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3285, a next-generation automatic movement delivering an impressive 70-hour power reserve, enhanced precision, and superior shock resistance. With its balanced design, modern movement, and consistent market demand, the 126710BLNR remains a top performer in both wearability and long-term value.

This 2024 example represents the latest evolution of a modern Rolex icon — a timepiece equally at home in the boardroom, the cockpit, or a serious investment portfolio.

ROLEX GMT Master II “Gold Bruce Wayne” Black Dial Yellow Gold Jubilee 126718GRNR

The ROLEX GMT Master II “Gold Bruce Wayne” reference 126718GRNR is the full yellow gold execution of Rolex’s newest black and grey bezel GMT configuration. Produced in 2025, this model features a black dial paired with the distinctive black and grey ceramic bezel that inspired the “Bruce Wayne” nickname among collectors. The watch is fitted on the iconic solid 18k yellow gold Jubilee bracelet, giving the classic GMT Master II travel watch an unmistakably bold and luxurious presence.

Among modern Rolex GMT references, the black and grey bezel configuration has quickly become one of the most discussed and sought-after releases. The “Gold Bruce Wayne” represents the most elevated version of that design, combining the understated monochrome bezel with the weight and presence of solid yellow gold. Collectors have gravitated toward this configuration due to its rarity, strong wrist presence, and connection to one of Rolex’s most recognizable tool watch lineages.

column1064:
One of the most talked-about modern GMTs Rolex has released, the “Sprite” flipped the script with its left-handed crown and green-black bezel. The black dial paired with the sharp two-tone Cerachrom insert gives it a look that stands apart from every other GMT in the lineup. Demand has stayed strong since launch, and this is still one of the harder steel sports models to secure at retail.

This reference features a 40mm Oystersteel case with a green and black Cerachrom bidirectional bezel and a black dial with Chromalight display and a green 24-hour hand. It runs on the automatic Caliber 3285 with approximately 70 hours of power reserve and Rolex’s Chronergy escapement. The watch is fitted on an Oyster bracelet in Oystersteel with an Oysterlock clasp and Easylink extension.

column1178:
The Rolex Oyster Precision remains one of the brand's most refined and "no frills" designs to this day. Going back several decades it is without a doubt one of Rolex' most beautiful, yet simple creations. Anyone with an average-sized wrist will find that the 34mm vintage case on the early references are as elegant on bracelet as they are strap.
This particular ref. 6426, circa 1972 remains in remarkable vintage condition. It boasts a perfectly aged dial with all of its tritium plots present, full and aged to a warm patina, matching the hands. The case remains extremely sharp with thick, evenly proportioned lugs. It retains its correct folded link bracelet that remains extremely tight and shows a strong coronet on its clasp. For any collector looking for a highly charming and simple Rolex, this example is a true survivor. Its manual-winding movement also allows this model to be thinner at just 8mm so it is equally well-suited for being dressed up on leather.

Rolex uses Oystersteel for its steel watch cases. Specially developed by the brand, Oystersteel belongs to the 904L steel family, alloys most commonly used in high-technology and in the aerospace and chemical industries, where maximum resistance to corrosion is essential. The Oyster bracelet is a perfect alchemy of form and function, aesthetics and technology.

column1189:
Rolex GMT-Master II “Batgirl” – Ref. 126710BLNR

Offered here is the Rolex GMT-Master II reference 126710BLNR, widely known as the “Batgirl,” one of the most iconic and enduring modern Rolex sports watches. Featuring the distinctive black and blue Cerachrom bezel and paired with the elegant Jubilee bracelet, this reference strikes a near-perfect balance between professional tool watch functionality and everyday refinement.

The 40mm Oystersteel case is exceptionally well finished, with crisp factory brushing and polished accents that highlight Rolex’s renowned attention to detail. The bidirectional Cerachrom bezel features a deep black and rich blue color split, with platinum-coated numerals that offer excellent contrast and long-term durability. This bezel design has become one of the most recognizable in the GMT-Master lineup.

The glossy black dial is clean and highly legible, fitted with luminous Chromalight hour markers and Mercedes hands for optimal visibility in all lighting conditions. The blue GMT hand allows the wearer to track a second time zone independently, a feature originally developed for international pilots and still perfectly suited to modern travel. The date aperture at 3 o’clock is magnified by Rolex’s Cyclops lens for effortless readability.

Powering the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3285, a next-generation automatic movement featuring the Chronergy escapement, Parachrom hairspring, and a robust 70-hour power reserve. Certified as a Superlative Chronometer, the movement exceeds standard chronometer specifications, delivering exceptional accuracy, shock resistance, and long-term reliability.

This example is fitted to the Jubilee bracelet, which combines five-piece links for enhanced comfort and a slightly more refined aesthetic compared to the Oyster bracelet. The bracelet is secured by a folding Oysterclasp with Oysterlock safety and Rolex’s Easylink extension system, allowing for quick micro-adjustments as wrist size changes throughout the day.

Details

Reference
column1190:
If you boil Rolex down to its purest form, what do you get?

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual. This is truly the genesis for the entire Rolex brand. The very first Rolex models were called ‘Oysters’ which was owed to their innovative three part case that all screwed into each-other. This watch represents an evolution of that, when these were still semi-bubble back watches.

The piece that we have here, simply know as an Oyster Perpetual, is a descendant of those early watches, though is probably not what first comes to mind when your hear Oyster Perpetual...

Dating to circa 1957, this Ref. 6599 is a 'jumbo' model, with a full-size 36mm case similar to a Datejust, rather than the more common 34mm diameter. Constructed out of solid yellow gold, it is offered in fantastic overall condition, with a seemingly unpolished case and 'fluted' yellow gold bezel. Inside, the sunburst silver dial exhibits some patina from age and features a nice symmetrical dial layout, with larger applied indices at the 3, 6, and 9 o'clock positions and elegant small text and is complimented by a matching 'dauphine' handset.

Furthermore, this piece is accompanied buy a box, chronometer papers, a COSC hangtag, and guarantee papers dating to 1959. It also bears an always alluring caseback engraving.

Despite being ‘entry-level’ these vintage OPs offer a ton of character and remain an overlooked part of the important history of Rolex on their journey to the powerhouse brand that they are today. And while we feel the 34mm variants are chronically slept on and present great value next to its more well-known siblings, these 36mm models are almost completely unknown!

Unworn 2025 Rolex Oyster Perpetual 36 – Ref. 126000-0006
A standout within Rolex’s most versatile collection, the Oyster Perpetual 36 Reference 126000-0006 is celebrated for its refined proportions and striking color palette. This 2025 release features the highly sought-after turquoise blue dial, a modern classic that evokes both freshness and sophistication. A collector's favorite since its debut, this configuration continues to enjoy strong demand for its vibrant yet balanced aesthetic.

The 36mm case is crafted from corrosion-resistant Oystersteel, ensuring lasting durability and a polished finish. Its smooth domed bezel complements the vivid turquoise dial, which features applied baton hour markers and Rolex’s signature Chromalight display with long-lasting blue luminescence. The Twinlock screw-down crown ensures water resistance to 100 meters.

At the heart of the watch is the Rolex Caliber 3230, a self-winding mechanical movement entirely developed and manufactured in-house. Offering a robust 70-hour power reserve, the caliber features Rolex’s patented Chronergy escapement and Paramagnetic blue Parachrom hairspring, enhancing its resistance to shocks and magnetic fields.

Fitted with the iconic Oyster bracelet, composed of flat three-piece links and secured with the Oysterclasp and Easylink 5mm extension, this watch offers both comfort and understated elegance.

column1212:
The Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most historic and iconic watches the brand ever produced. Its origin dates back to a commission request by Pan American World Airways in the early 50s for their fleet of pilots who needed access to different time zones.

The watch would feature a similar design to another watch that Rolex was developing around that time, the Submariner, but would include a fourth hand running on 24-hr time, and a bi-colored bezel insert to represent AM/PM times. Those colors, naturally, would be the PanAm colors
column1213:
Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 – Ref. 134300 (Blue Dial)

Offered here is the Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 reference 134300, presented with the highly desirable blue dial. This reference represents the purest expression of Rolex design, understated, perfectly proportioned, and engineered to the same uncompromising standards as the brand’s professional models.

The 41mm Oystersteel case features a smooth, polished bezel and beautifully balanced lines, making it one of the most versatile watches in the Rolex catalog. Crafted from Oystersteel, a proprietary alloy developed by Rolex, the case offers exceptional resistance to corrosion while maintaining a refined, luxurious finish. The proportions of the 134300 wear exceptionally well on the wrist, combining modern presence with classic restraint.

The sunburst blue dial is vibrant yet elegant, shifting subtly in tone depending on the light. Applied baton hour markers and hands are filled with Chromalight lume, providing excellent legibility in low-light conditions. The minimalist dial layout, free of complications beyond the time display, highlights Rolex’s commitment to clarity, balance, and timeless design.

Powering the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3230, a state-of-the-art automatic movement featuring the Chronergy escapement, Parachrom hairspring, and a 70-hour power reserve. As a Superlative Chronometer, the movement is regulated to Rolex’s stringent accuracy standards, ensuring outstanding precision, shock resistance, and long-term reliability.

The watch is fitted to the solid Oyster bracelet with brushed links and a robust Oysterclasp, providing both comfort and durability for everyday wear. The clasp incorporates Rolex’s Easylink extension system, allowing for quick micro-adjustments to accommodate wrist changes throughout the day.

column1264:
Rolex Sea-Dweller Deepsea Challenge RLX Titanium 50mm 126067 New

This watch is unworn, comes with a box and all the original papers. Ready to ship.

The horizon of the deep. The Oyster Perpetual Deepsea Challenge in RLX titanium with a Cerachrom bezel insert in black ceramic and an Oyster bracelet.

Ceramic Bezel and Luminescent Display
Legendary divers’ watch
The 60-minute graduated, unidirectional rotatable bezel of the Deepsea Challenge enables divers to precisely and safely monitor their dive and decompression times.

It is equipped with a patented black Cerachrom bezel insert manufactured by Rolex in a virtually scratchproof ceramic whose colour is unaffected by ultraviolet rays. The graduation is coated via PVD (Physical Vapour Deposition) with a thin layer of platinum. The dial features large Chromalight hour markers and hands, filled with luminescent material that emits a long-lasting blue glow for excellent legibility in dark conditions.

Intense black dial
For legibility underwater
The dial’s luminescent Chromalight display is an innovation that improved visibility in dark environments, an essential feature for divers.

Hour markers in simple shapes – triangles, circles, rectangles – and broad hour and minute hands enable instant and reliable reading to prevent any risk of confusion underwater.

RLX titanium
Ultralight resistance
RLX titanium is a grade 5 titanium alloy selected by Rolex for its weightlessness and resistance to deformation and corrosion. Although insensitive to abyssal pressure, the experimental watch that accompanied James Cameron in the Mariana Trench still posed a wearability problem due to its weight, as it was made of 904L steel.

The use of RLX titanium to create the case and bracelet of the Deepsea Challenge considerably lightened its weight

column1269:
The Rolex Sky‑Dweller 326934 Blue Dial Jubilee is one of the most popular and recognizable modern Rolex watches, combining an elegant look with advanced complications designed for travelers.

Description

The Rolex Sky-Dweller reference 326934 features a 42 mm Oyster case crafted from White Rolesor, which is Rolex’s combination of Oystersteel and an 18k white-gold fluted bezel. The watch stands out with its bright sunburst blue dial, widely considered the most desirable dial color in the Sky-Dweller collection. ￼

The dial layout is unique to the Sky-Dweller. It includes an off-center 24-hour disc that shows a second time zone, allowing the wearer to track home time while traveling. Around the outer edge of the dial are 12 small windows that indicate the month, part of Rolex’s patented Saros annual calendar system. ￼

This model is fitted with the Jubilee bracelet, a five-link stainless-steel bracelet known for its comfort and dressier appearance compared to the Oyster bracelet.

Inside the watch is the Rolex Caliber 9001, one of Rolex’s most complex movements. It is a self-winding mechanical movement with a 72-hour power reserve and allows the wearer to adjust the watch easily using the Ring Command bezel, which controls the different functions when rotated. ￼

Key Features

Unworn 2025 Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 – Ref. 134300-0008
The Rolex Oyster Perpetual 41 Reference 134300-0008 is a bold continuation of a classic. Released in 2025, this model refines the Oyster Perpetual collection with subtle design enhancements and upgraded movement technology. Featuring a timeless black dial, this piece embodies the perfect balance of sophistication and everyday versatility, making it a cornerstone in the modern Rolex lineup.

The watch features a 41mm Oystersteel case, renowned for its exceptional durability and resistance to corrosion. A smooth domed bezel gives the watch a clean, polished profile, while the sunray-finished black dial is accentuated with baton-style hour markers and a Chromalight display, providing outstanding legibility day or night.

At its heart is the Rolex Caliber 7140, a new-generation automatic movement introduced with this reference. It offers a 66-hour power reserve, enhanced efficiency via the Chronergy escapement, and high resistance to magnetic fields and shocks through the Syloxi silicon hairspring. The movement is COSC-certified and Superlative Chronometer rated, ensuring precise and reliable timekeeping.

The watch is fitted with the signature Oyster bracelet, a robust three-piece link design in Oystersteel, paired with the Oysterclasp and Easylink comfort extension, providing a seamless wearing experience.

column1289:
The Rolex Datejust is one of the most significant models in the history of watchmaking. First introduced in 1945, it was the first self-winding wristwatch to display the date on the dial. The Datejust quickly became a symbol of prestige and luxury, and has been worn by some of the world's most influential figures, including US President Eisenhower and Martin Luther King Jr. Over the years, Rolex has continued to refine the design of the Datejust, adding new features and innovations while staying true to its classic look and feel. Today, the Datejust remains one of the most popular and recognizable watches in the world, a testament to its timeless design and enduring appeal.
This five digit reference example is a W46X serial, circa '95 and benefits from the addition of a quickset date function. This piece houses a beautiful silver dial that has "tropicalized" to a remarkable "bronze" tone, adding unique character and warmth. The bezel remains sharp. The bezel remains sharp and the case retains thick, even lugs. The bracelet remains displays medium stretch and will fit an approximate 6.75 inch wrist. For anyone looking for a practical daily driver with character, this neo-vintage example hits all the right notes.

At its core beats the Rolex Caliber 3285, a state-of-the-art automatic movement offering approximately 70 hours of power reserve, enhanced efficiency, and chronometer-certified precision. column1359:
If you boil Rolex down to its purest form, what do you get?

The Rolex Oyster Perpetual. This is truly the genesis for the entire Rolex brand. The very first Rolex models were called ‘Oysters’ which was owed to their innovative three part case that all screwed into each-other. That is where the fluted bezel actually derives from, being screwed down into the top of the case to secure the crystal with a gasket - as well as the grooves on the oyster case back. All makes sense now - right?

The piece that we have here, called an Oyster, is a descendant of those early watches, utilizing a water-resistant Oyster case, an manual-winding movement, and nothing else. No frills — purely a functional timekeeping device.

The Reference 6022 that we have here, dates to circa 1952 and is nothing short of a beautiful timepiece. The stainless steel Oyster case comes in at 34mm in diameter and comes to us in excellent overall condition, with thick and symmetrical proportions and showing normal signs of wear from age and use. The silver dial within has developed a gentle patina across the surface and shows light wear consistent with age. It features applied yellow gold-tone 'Arabic' indices, which are complimented by a matching yellow gold-tone 'feuille' handset.

As if all of these beautiful period details weren't enough, this piece also bears an additional signature on the top half that reads "A.&J. Smith Aberdeen" — a London-based retailer of watches & clocks.

Despite being ‘entry-level’ these offer a ton of character and remain an overlooked part of the important history of Rolex on their journey to the powerhouse brand that they are today. This is nothing short of a delightful vintage piece that can be worn every single day, on any occasion, and on virtually any wrist.

And don't let the case size fool you, these 34mm Rolex models are chronically slept on and present great value next to its more well-known siblings, while truly embodying the essence of Rolex.

column1366:
The Rolex Submariner is a true icon — and the word "icon" does get thrown around an awful lot. If you were to ask ten people at random to draw you their idea of what a watch looks like, there is a significant chance that they would draw or describe something that looks like a Rolex Submariner. It’s the dive watch of reference, and good/bad/indifferent there is no escaping it. Throughout its 70-year production, there have been countless small changes made to the model, particularly in the first decade or so, with the 5513 representing a true landmark in the history of the model.

The Ref. 5513 mirrored the slightly earlier Ref. 5512 in virtually every way, minus the chronometer certified calibre within, and therefore only 2 lines of text on the bottom half of the dial, rather than the 4 that chronometer certified watches bear until this day. The 5513 enjoyed a long twenty-seven-year production run, and like many Rolex models, there was a large number of variations to the dials, bezel inserts, bracelets, etc., which results in a very fun and interesting collecting experience.

The piece that we have here dates to circa 1978 and features what is known within the collecting community as a 'MK1 Maxi' dial. The case shows normal signs of wear throughout, having been lightly polished in the past and retains thick and symmetrical lugs. The correct solid-link Oyster bracelet is seemingly unpolished and shows matching wear. Inside, that 'Maxi' dial with it's oversized plots is beautifully preserved and seemingly devoid of any wear. The Tritium plots exhibit an attractive, almost gradient patina throughout, and the handset matches beautifully. The black aluminum bezel insert is also in excellent condition and has developed a 'matte' appearance over the years, and the Tritium pip within matches the dial and hands nicely.

For many of us, this is what vintage is all about

This Sea-Dweller Deepsea is a purpose-built professional dive watch engineered for extreme underwater performance. Featuring a bold 44mm stainless steel case, black dial, and ceramic dive bezel, the Deepsea is instantly recognizable for its robust construction and commanding wrist presence. Designed with Rolex’s Ringlock System, this model offers an impressive water resistance rating of 3,900 meters, making it one of the most capable dive watches ever produced by the brand. column1397:
The Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most historic and iconic watches the brand ever produced. Its origin dates back to a commission request by Pan American World Airways in the early 50s for their fleet of pilots who needed access to different time zones.

These pieces would feature a similar design to another watch that Rolex was developing around that time, the Submariner, but would include a fourth hand running on 24-hr time, and a bi-colored bezel insert to represent AM/PM times. Those colors, naturally, would be the PanAm colors

Crafted entirely from 950 platinum, this timepiece is a masterpiece that celebrates the 60th anniversary of the legendary Daytona collection. The striking ice-blue dial—a signature reserved for Rolex’s platinum models—pairs perfectly with the chestnut brown Cerachrom bezel, creating a captivating contrast that exudes understated elegance.

Inside beats the Rolex Calibre 4131, a next-generation chronograph movement offering a 72-hour power reserve, superior shock resistance, and exceptional accuracy. Designed for collectors who demand nothing but the best, the 126506 is as technically impressive as it is visually stunning.

The "Platona" nickname, cherished by enthusiasts, reflects both its platinum construction and its cult-status among watch connoisseurs. Whether worn on the wrist or secured in a collection, this Daytona represents a pinnacle of modern Rolex watchmaking.

column1441:
The Rolex Perpetual 1908, reference 52508-0006, represents a bold new chapter in Rolex’s history of classic watchmaking. Introduced as part of the Perpetual collection, the 1908 draws inspiration from early Rolex designs while embodying the brand’s contemporary mastery of elegance, innovation, and precision. With its slim profile, refined detailing, and luxurious materials, the 1908 has quickly become a collector’s highlight and a symbol of understated sophistication.

The 39mm case is crafted from solid 18k yellow gold, combining timeless appeal with Rolex’s renowned craftsmanship. Powering the watch is the in-house Caliber 7140, a slim self-winding movement developed specifically for the 1908. It features a skeletonized oscillating weight in 18k yellow gold, a Syloxi silicon hairspring, and a 66-hour power reserve, ensuring superior accuracy and reliability. The polished domed bezel frames the elegant dial with small seconds at 6 o’clock, while the sapphire caseback — a rare feature for Rolex — reveals the finely decorated movement within. The watch is fitted with a hand-stitched black alligator leather strap, secured with an 18k yellow gold dual folding clasp, offering a perfect blend of luxury and comfort.

column1470:
Rolex Sea-Dweller Deepsea “James Cameron” – Ref. 116660 (2016)

The Rolex Sea-Dweller Deepsea “James Cameron” is a modern icon of professional dive watch engineering, built to conquer the most extreme environments on Earth. Inspired by filmmaker and explorer James Cameron’s historic solo dive to the Challenger Deep, this 2016 reference blends uncompromising performance with unmistakable Rolex prestige.

Crafted from 904L Oystersteel, the 44mm case houses Rolex’s patented Ringlock System, enabling an extraordinary 3,900-meter (12,800-foot) water resistance—a benchmark unmatched by most mechanical watches. The striking D-Blue gradient dial, transitioning from deep blue to black, visually represents the descent into the ocean’s depths and has become one of Rolex’s most recognizable modern designs.

At its heart beats the Rolex Caliber 3135, a COSC-certified Superlative Chronometer known for exceptional reliability, precision, and durability. From the robust Cerachrom bezel to the Glidelock clasp for precise fit over a wetsuit, every element is engineered for real-world performance while maintaining luxury-grade refinement.

Highly sought after by collectors and professionals alike, the Deepsea “James Cameron” stands as both a technical milestone and a long-term collectible—making it a compelling addition to any serious investment portfolio.

column1501:
Rolex - Submariner Date “Kermit” - 16610LV

The Rolex Submariner Date “Kermit” (Reference 16610LV) stands as one of the most celebrated modern classics in Rolex’s history. Released in 2003 to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the Submariner, this model introduced a bold departure from tradition with its striking green aluminum bezel—earning it the nickname “Kermit” among collectors.

This reference retains the timeless design language of the Submariner line, featuring a 40mm stainless steel case, black “Maxi” dial with larger hour markers, and the robust Oyster bracelet that ensures both comfort and durability. Beneath the surface, the watch is powered by Rolex’s reliable Caliber 3135 automatic movement, known for its precision and longevity.

The “Kermit” is more than a tool watch—it’s a symbol of Rolex’s evolution, blending heritage with innovation. Its limited production period (2003–2010) has made it one of the most sought-after Submariners among collectors and enthusiasts worldwide.

column1510:
The 1970s were an interesting time for the watch industry. The Great Quartz Crisis from Japan offered the world better quality watches, that were more accurate, more reliable, and generally much more affordable. A kiss of death for mechanical watchmaking at the time. Even the very best of Switzerland will still lose accuracy over time — an issue that a quartz watch virtually solved (though not perfect). Great, beloved Swiss brands like Universal Genève, Gallet, Zodiac, and Blancpain would disappear — some of which were acquired by holding companies, other brands, etc. and eventually resurface, but hundreds of smaller brands were completely wiped away.

Rolex responded by producing their own version of the quartz watch called it the Oysterquartz, which introduced a more style-forward and angular design that was becoming popular in the 1970s, and of course, a quartz movement inside. On top of this new model line name, these pieces utilized existing cornerstone model names to denote specific models with the Oysterquartz range, such as the Datejust and Day-Date.

By the middle of the 1980s, the Swiss watch industry was able to pivot and re-establish itself as a luxury product worthy of higher prices that could justify the cost and complexity of designing and manufacturing a mechanical watch. So, the Oysterquartz is massively important in the history of Rolex, representing the only time in its nearly 120-years of business that it was ever under siege in a truly threatening way. These did not stay in the Rolex lineup for long and are therefore surprisingly unusual to come by.

The piece that we have here is a Day-Date variant dating to circa 1979, and as with all other pieces in Rolex's flagship line, it is constructed out of solid gold. Inside, it has a very of-its-time, lacquered 'burlwood' dial — further adding to this pieces scarcity. The case and integrated bracelet are in fantastic overall condition, seemingly unpolished and showing only light signs of wear throughout. The characteristic 'fluted' yellow gold bezel retains crisp finishing and also shows only light signs of wear. Inside, the non-luminous lacquered 'burlwood' dial with its printed minute markers, applied indices, and framed day & date apertures remains in outstanding, seemingly-flawless condition and is complimented by a gently patinated Tritium handset. The 'flat-link' bracelet wears beautifully on the wrist and features a signed 'hidden' deployant clasp.

This then, is a strange yet important piece from the pages of Rolex's history, and is a must if you are looking to add a Day-Date to a collection of many, or simply snag a lesser-known piece thats a bit out of the ordinary.

column1520:
Rolex Sea-Dweller 126600 “Red Writing” – Description

The Rolex Sea-Dweller 126600, often called the “Red Writing” or “Red Line” Sea-Dweller, is one of Rolex’s most powerful modern dive watches. Introduced in 2017 for the 50th anniversary of the Sea-Dweller, it features the iconic single line of red “Sea-Dweller” text on the dial—a tribute to the original vintage models from 1967. ￼

The watch is crafted from Oystersteel with a bold 43mm case, making it larger and more robust than the Submariner. It features a black dial with Chromalight luminescent markers, a black Cerachrom ceramic bezel with platinum-coated graduations, and a Cyclops lens over the date—the first time a Sea-Dweller included the Cyclops magnifier. ￼

Inside the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3235 automatic movement, delivering exceptional accuracy and a 70-hour power reserve. Built for professional saturation diving, the Sea-Dweller includes a helium escape valve and is water-resistant to 1,220 meters (4,000 feet). ￼

The watch is fitted with the classic Oyster bracelet with Oysterlock clasp and Glidelock extension system, allowing easy micro-adjustments and the ability to fit over a diving suit. ￼

The watch measures approximately 26mm in diameter, 13mm in lug width, 32.50mm from lug tip to lug tip, and 10.50mm in case thickness. column1528:
The Rolex Datejust is one of the most significant models in the history of watchmaking. First introduced in 1945, it was the first self-winding wristwatch to display the date on the dial. The Datejust quickly became a symbol of prestige and luxury, and has been worn by some of the world's most influential figures, including US President Eisenhower and Martin Luther King Jr. Over the years, Rolex has continued to refine the design of the Datejust, adding new features and innovations while staying true to its classic look and feel. Today, the Datejust remains one of the most popular and recognizable watches in the world, a testament to its timeless design and enduring appeal.
This five digit reference example is a A15X serial, circa '99 and benefits from the addition of a quickset date function. This piece houses a beautiful silver dial that has remained in mint condition. The bezel shows some light wear, as does the case which retains thick and even lugs. The bracelet remains displays light stretch and retains a strong coronet on the clasp. It will fit an approximate 7.25 inch wrist. For anyone looking for the perfect daily driver, this neo-vintage Datejust boast stunning preservation and features Super-LumiNova, for easy legibility at night. It also just came back from a complete service and retains previous service documentation from Rolex Service Center.

Dial
column1529:
The Rolex Submariner Date ref. 16610 remains one of the most iconic dive watches ever made, and this 2008 example represents the last era of the classic aluminum bezel before Rolex transitioned to ceramic. It features a 40 mm stainless steel case, engraved rehaut, and a timeless black dial with luminous hour markers for excellent legibility. Powered by the automatic Caliber 3135, this Submariner delivers robust timekeeping and a 48-hour power reserve. Recently serviced, it’s running smoothly and ready to wear. Offered as watch only, this piece is a great opportunity to own a well-maintained example of a late-production Sub Date at a strong value.

Introducing the unworn 2025 Rolex Oyster Perpetual 31 Ref. 277200-0017, an elegant timepiece that reflects Rolex’s heritage of timeless sophistication and modern precision. Crafted from Rolex’s signature Oystersteel, the 31mm case combines durability with sleek aesthetics, enhanced by a polished smooth bezel that highlights its understated luxury. The captivating blue dial sets this model apart with its contemporary yet classic character. Applied baton-style markers and polished silver-tone hands are filled with Rolex’s Chromalight luminescent material, emitting a striking blue glow that ensures legibility in all lighting conditions. A finely printed minute track frames the dial, emphasizing clarity and balance. Protected by a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal and closed with a solid Oystersteel screw-down case back, the watch is engineered for both durability and elegance. At its core, the Rolex Calibre 2232 beats with exceptional accuracy, featuring 31 jewels, a 55-hour power reserve, and stop-seconds for precise time setting. Certified as a Superlative Chronometer, it guarantees top-level performance in any environment. The classic Oyster bracelet in Oystersteel completes the watch, with three-piece solid links offering strength, comfort, and a secure fit. column1576:
Rolex Submariner “Bluesy” 126613LB Description

The Rolex Submariner Date 126613LB, famously nicknamed the “Bluesy,” is one of the most recognizable luxury dive watches in the Rolex lineup. This striking model combines rugged sport functionality with bold two-tone elegance.

Crafted in 41mm Yellow Rolesor—a blend of Oystersteel and 18k yellow gold—the watch features a vibrant sunburst blue dial paired with a matching blue Cerachrom ceramic bezel. The bezel is unidirectional with 60-minute graduations, allowing divers to track immersion time safely. ￼

The dial is equipped with large Chromalight luminescent hour markers and hands, ensuring exceptional readability in low-light conditions. At 3 o’clock, the iconic Cyclops lens magnifies the date for quick and easy viewing. ￼

Powering the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3235 automatic movement, offering outstanding precision and an approximate 70-hour power reserve. Built with Rolex’s Oyster architecture and Triplock crown, the Submariner is water-resistant to 300 meters (1,000 feet), making it a true professional dive watch. ￼

The watch is fitted with the classic two-tone Oyster bracelet featuring polished yellow-gold center links and a secure Oysterlock clasp with Glidelock extension, allowing easy adjustment without tools. ￼

The Sky-Dweller is one of Rolex's most complicated watches, featuring a dual time zone display. The central hands indicate the local time, while a rotating 24-hour off-center disk displays a secondary time zone, making it perfect for travelers.

column1614:
The Rolex Submariner is a true icon — and the word "icon" does get thrown around an awful lot. If you were to ask ten people at random to draw you their idea of what a watch looks like, there is a significant chance that they would draw or describe something that looks like a Rolex Submariner. It’s the dive watch of reference, and good/bad/indifferent there is no escaping it. Throughout its 70-year production, there have been countless small changes made to the model, particularly in the first decade or so, and this Ref. 16618 has its own little place in the history of the model.

The Ref. 1680 was the first Submariner to feature a date, and had a long production run spanning close to 15 years - and was eventually replaced by the 16800 series of Submariner Date. During that 16800 era, the watch underwent a series of changes that saw the early models born with matte dials, while the later ones were born with glossy dials with white gold surrounds. As was the case with the Ref. 1680, the 5-digit also received the solid yellow gold treatment.

The example that we have here, a neo-vintage Reference 16618, dates to circa 1991 and comes to us in fantastic overall condition. The case shows signs of a light and tasteful previous polishing, seemingly only to the case profiles, as it retains flawless hallmarks and a fully-intact caseback sticker. The blue dial is virtually flawless, with crisp and vibrant sunburst finishing, evenly patinated Tritium-filled yellow gold indices, and a matching handset.

It could be argued that a yellow gold GMT-Master makes sense, but a solid gold dive watch? There is no point. And that is exactly what makes it great.

The Rolex Submariner 5513 is one of the most iconic and collectible vintage dive watches ever produced, celebrated for its clean no-date dial and pure tool-watch design. This reference features a robust 40mm stainless steel Oyster case paired with a classic black aluminum bezel, creating a perfectly balanced and purposeful presence on the wrist. The black dial, offered in matte or glossy finishes depending on production year, is fitted with luminous hour markers and hands for excellent legibility in all conditions.

This model is powered by Rolex’s automatic Caliber 1520 or 1530 movement, known for its durability and long-term reliability. Unlike later chronometer-certified Submariners, the 5513 retains a non-chronometer movement, which adds to its vintage charm and historical significance. Its long production run and strong collector demand have made it a cornerstone piece for serious vintage watch enthusiasts.

column1673:
The Rolex Air-King is one of the most quietly significant yet undervalued models in the brand’s lineup. Originally introduced in the 1940s to honor RAF pilots, it represents a pure expression of Rolex’s Oyster heritage and time-only simplicity, balanced proportions, and everyday reliability. The 34mm case is often overlooked in favor of larger sport models, but as preferences shift toward more refined and wearable sizing, its proportions feel increasingly relevant. For decades, 34mm was a standard for men’s Rolex watches, and the smooth bezel, clean dial, and understated presence give the AK a timeless elegance. Seen in recent years on the wrist of actor Ryan Gosling, the model underscores how confident and versatile a smaller case can be.
This particular T74X serial example, circa '96 houses an "Explorer" type dial with Arabic numerals at 3, 6, & 9 o-clock, now coveted by collectors. Interestingly, some of these dials have aged differently and this blue piece on offer has aged to a lighter tone than most we've come across. Overall, it has remained in excellent neo-vintage condition with notable dial wear, a sharp case retaining a caseback sticker and tight bracelet retaining 12 links. It will fit an approximate 7.25 inch wrist. For the discerning collector, this piece also comes accompanied by warranty papers that notate the blue Arabic dial.

column1685:
The Rolex Submariner was first introduced by Rolex in 1953 and is notable for being one of the first watches specifically designed for diving. Beyond its technical capabilities, the Submariner has become an icon in the world of watches, with a timeless design that has remained largely unchanged since its introduction. It is widely regarded as one of the most important and influential watches of all time, and has become a symbol of Rolex's reputation for quality, durability, and precision. The Sub has been worn by everyone from professional divers and military personnel to Hollywood celebrities and heads of state, cementing its place in the pantheon of iconic watches.
The ref. 16610 really brings everything loved about the vintage Submariner into the modern age into what is now considered a modern classic. This particular Z97X example, circa 2006-7 is one of the very final examples produced before the engraved rehaut as this feature was added in late Z serial examples. For us, this is an interesting "transitional" example given the more classic blank rehaut and the addition of a warranty card. This piece remains in excellent condition with a mint dial, well-preserved insert and very strong looking case/bracelet. For the discerning collector, this piece comes as a true full set which includes inner and outer boxes, warranty papers, serialized and wax tag, booklets, wallet, and anchor. If you've been looking for the perfect daily driver, this reference is one of our top recommendations.

column1722:
Rolex Sky-Dweller – Champagne Dial – 326933 – 2020

A bold expression of technical mastery and refined luxury, the Rolex Sky-Dweller reference 326933 stands as one of Rolex’s most sophisticated creations. Designed for global travelers and discerning collectors alike, this model seamlessly blends advanced functionality with unmistakable Rolex elegance.

Crafted in Rolesor—a combination of Oystersteel and 18k yellow gold—this Sky-Dweller features a warm champagne dial that perfectly complements its two-tone Oyster bracelet, delivering a powerful yet versatile presence on the wrist. The fluted yellow gold bezel is not only iconic but also functional, serving as the control interface for Rolex’s innovative Ring Command system.

At the heart of this timepiece is Rolex’s annual calendar complication, which automatically differentiates between 30- and 31-day months, requiring adjustment only once per year. The off-center 24-hour disc allows the wearer to track a second time zone effortlessly, making this watch as practical as it is luxurious.

column1739:
The Rolex Explorer II 16550 is one of the most important transitional models in Rolex history, blending vintage character with early signs of the modern Explorer II design. Produced for a short period in the mid-1980s, the 16550 introduced a number of significant upgrades to the Explorer line, making it a highly collectible reference today.

This example features the black factory service dial, an official Rolex replacement that preserves authenticity while offering a crisp, clean appearance. The luminous hands and markers provide excellent legibility, and the dial layout remains faithful to the iconic Explorer II aesthetic.

Powered by the Rolex Caliber 3085, the 16550 offers independently adjustable hour-hand functionality—an early evolution of the modern GMT-style feature. The 40mm stainless steel case delivers excellent wrist presence while maintaining classic proportions. The fixed 24-hour steel bezel is designed for easy readability and durability, and the sapphire crystal brings a more modern feel compared to earlier acrylic models.

The 16550 has earned strong collector interest for its rarity, transitional details, and dependable construction. Whether worn daily or added to a curated collection, it represents one of the most meaningful Explorer II references ever produced.

The Rolex Cosmograph Daytona ref. 116523 Mother of Pearl is a striking blend of sport and luxury, combining Rolex’s legendary chronograph heritage with an elegant two-tone design. Crafted in Oystersteel and 18k yellow gold, the watch features a 40mm case paired with a solid 18k yellow gold tachymeter bezel, designed to measure average speeds and reinforce the Daytona’s racing roots. ￼

Its standout feature is the natural mother-of-pearl dial, which shimmers with unique tones that change depending on the light. Many versions are fitted with factory diamond hour markers, adding an extra level of sophistication while maintaining the Daytona’s iconic chronograph layout with three gold-outlined subdials for the seconds, 30-minute, and 12-hour counters. ￼

Powering the watch is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 4130 automatic chronograph movement, a highly reliable and precise movement known for its durability and smooth chronograph operation. The watch is finished with a two-tone Oyster bracelet in stainless steel and 18k yellow gold with a secure Oysterlock clasp, providing both comfort and durability. ￼

Protected by a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal and water-resistant to 100 meters, the Daytona 116523 MOP is a perfect combination of performance and refined style—making it one of the most eye-catching two-tone Daytonas ever produced. ￼

column1795:
The Rolex Air-King is one of the most quietly significant yet undervalued models in the brand’s lineup. Originally introduced in the 1940s to honor RAF pilots, it represents a pure expression of Rolex’s Oyster heritage and time-only simplicity, balanced proportions, and everyday reliability. The 34mm case is often overlooked in favor of larger sport models, but as preferences shift toward more refined and wearable sizing, its proportions feel increasingly relevant. For decades, 34mm was a standard for men’s Rolex watches, and the smooth bezel, clean dial, and understated presence give the AK a timeless elegance. Seen in recent years on the wrist of actor Ryan Gosling, the model underscores how confident and versatile a smaller case can be.
This particular X68X serial example, circa '91 houses a silver dial that has taken a stunning "tropical pink" patina throughout, adding warmth and subtle character. This piece has remained in excellent neo-vintage condition with a very well-preserved dial/hands and strong case with clean, sharp edges. The bracelet is tight, retains 12 links and will fit an approximate 7.25 inch wrist. For the discerning collector, this piece also comes accompanied by warranty papers.

NAMED ONE OF WATCHPRO MAGAZINE’S “HOT 100” - THE TOP 100 INFLUENCERS OF THE U.S. WATCH INDUSTRY. HOW CAN WE SERVE YOU?

1946 ROLEX PRECISION Vintage Mens Claw Lugs Square Dress Watch - 14K Gold & Stainless Steel

Rare & Classic - Beautiful Restored Condition - Claw Lugs - 1 Year Warranty

In the 1930's through the 50's, in the midst of their high output of utilitarian Oyster sports watches, Rolex also made much more elegant dress watches. These fine wristwatches often combine a superior hand-wound mechanism with beautiful aesthetics.

The elegant and sophisticated Rolex Precision showcased in today's offering is a very fine example of this phenomenon, likely made shortly after the end of World War II. It features pleasing styling and a well-executed handwound movement.

We really love the aesthetic of this watch with its solid gold case top with distinctive claw lugs, thin bezel, and classic dial. Its large dial and small bezel make it wear even larger than its dimensions.

Its original gold "feuille" hands and gold Arabic numerals all elevate its elegance.

Powering the watch is its original Rolex caliber 700 movement with "Super Balance" system adjusted to two positions. We have owned many dozens of caliber 700 powered Rolexes from this era - this one is shockingly clean.

It has all of its original parts with the only possible exception of the crown, which is period correct but likely a replacement.

column1857:
The Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most historic and iconic watches the brand ever produced. Its origin dates back to a commission request by Pan American World Airways in the early 50’s for their fleet of pilots who needed access to different time zones.

These pieces would feature a similar design to another watch that Rolex was developing around that time, the Submariner, but would include a fourth hand running on 24-hr time, and a bi-colored bezel insert to represent AM/PM times. Those colors, naturally, would be the PanAm colors

The Rolex Explorer II Ref. 16570 is a respected tool watch from Rolex’s professional sports collection, designed for durability, precision, and legibility in extreme environments. This reference features a 40mm stainless steel case paired with a crisp black dial, offering a bold and highly functional aesthetic that has made the Explorer II a favorite among enthusiasts and collectors. The fixed 24-hour bezel and distinctive red GMT hand allow the wearer to track a second time zone or distinguish between day and night, a feature originally developed for cave explorers and adventurers.

Powered by the reliable Rolex Caliber 3185 automatic movement, the Explorer II delivers dependable timekeeping and robust performance. The dial features luminous hour markers and Mercedes-style hands for excellent readability in low-light conditions, along with a practical date display at 3 o’clock beneath a sapphire crystal with Rolex’s Cyclops magnifier. Completed with a stainless steel Oyster bracelet, the Explorer II 16570 balances rugged functionality with the classic design language that has defined Rolex professional watches for decades.

column1884:
The Rolex GMT‑Master II 126720VTNR “Sprite” on the Oyster bracelet is one of Rolex’s most distinctive modern sport watches. It was introduced in 2022 and quickly became highly sought-after because of its unusual left-handed design and unique green-and-black bezel.

Description

The Rolex GMT-Master II “Sprite” features a 40 mm Oystersteel case with a black dial and a bi-color green and black Cerachrom ceramic bezel. The color combination inspired collectors to nickname the watch “Sprite,” similar to how other GMT models are called “Pepsi” or “Batman.” ￼

What makes this model unique is its left-handed (destro) configuration—the crown and date window are positioned on the left side of the case, a first for the GMT-Master II collection. ￼

The watch is powered by the Rolex Caliber 3285, a self-winding mechanical movement that provides dual time-zone functionality through the GMT hand and offers approximately 70 hours of power reserve with Superlative Chronometer accuracy. ￼

This version comes on the Oyster bracelet, a three-link stainless-steel bracelet known for its durability and sporty look, equipped with the Oysterlock clasp and Easylink extension system for added comfort. ￼

column1933:
Rolex - Daytona - Black Dial - 126503

A timeless blend of sport and sophistication, the Rolex Cosmograph Daytona 126503 embodies the perfect harmony between performance and prestige. Crafted in Oystersteel and 18k yellow gold, this two-tone masterpiece elevates one of Rolex’s most iconic chronographs to a new level of refinement.

The sleek black dial enhances the watch’s professional aesthetic, featuring contrasting gold sub-dials that highlight the chronograph function with striking clarity. The tachymetric bezel, a hallmark of the Daytona collection, allows drivers to measure average speeds with precision — a nod to its enduring motorsport heritage.

On the wrist, the two-tone Oyster bracelet delivers a perfect balance of comfort and distinction, offering the strength of steel with the radiance of gold. Powered by Rolex’s Calibre 4131, this self-winding movement ensures exceptional chronometric performance, durability, and reliability under any condition.

This model captures the spirit of endurance racing while remaining unmistakably elegant — a statement of luxury and legacy for those who demand excellence in every detail.

column1937:
Introduced in 1954, the now cult classic Rolex GMT was initially designed as a pilots and travelers watch with its roots tied to Pan Am. The first examples featured a "Pepsi" bezel insert which provided ease of use for deciphering day/night in an alternate time-zone and remains in production to this day. Over the decades, various iterations of the GMT have been released from the "Root Beer" to the blue "Batman." Today, the GMT is as practical as it is popular with collectors, offering great beauty in it's vintage and neo-vintage examples which have taken on their own unique character over the decades.
This particular GMT ref. 16700, circa 1999 has remained in beautiful neo-vintage condition. Not only is it a final production year example, it comes as a true full collector set, making it a compelling example. The dial is mint and the insert is very close to being "as new" aside from some very light wear that is not distracting. The case has been polished in the past but retains good dimensions. The bracelet shows a matching date code, remains tight and retains 11 links. It comes as a complete set, including inner and outer boxes, warranty papers (that are open and devoid of a previous clients name), matching booklets, wallet, serialized and wax hang tags. Its hard to beat the thinner proportions of the ref. 16700 and we're thrilled to offer this stunning example in the shop.

Oyster Perpetual Cosmograph Daytona in 18 kt yellow gold with a black dial and contrasting counter rings. It features an Oyster bracelet and an 18 kt yellow gold bezel with an
engraved tachymetric scale.

With its tachymetric scale, its three counters and its pushers, the Cosmograph Daytona was designed to be the ultimate timing tool for endurance racing drivers.

The bezel features a tachymetric scale to read average speed over a given distance based on elapsed time.
This scale offers optimal legibility, making the Cosmograph Daytona the ideal instrument for measuring speeds up to 400 units per hour, expressed in kilometres or miles.

Model case:
