All Rolex watches sold by us come with the Original Box, Green Chronometer Tag, booklets, and Warranty Card (if stated that “papers” are included in the listing information). The Warranty Card will be named and dated if applicable. Stickers will have been removed at the time of purchase from the Authorized Rolex Dealer, unless otherwise stated. The watch may be sized from the time of purchase however it has never been worn (if condition stated as “Unworn/New”). It may also come with the white serial tag, but that is not guaranteed as some Authorized Dealers keep the tag. Because we often stock several of the same model watch, even if the watch is pictured with the white serial tag, it is not guaranteed to come with it. The watch may also be newer than noted on our listing. If we list as a 2021 Unworn, you may receive a 2022 Unworn. It is possible that the pictured watch in the ad has “stickers” on the watch, but you will receive a watch with stickers removed unless otherwise stated in the listing. You may receive a watch that has been wrapped in plastic by us, to prevent scratches during transit. Bezel protectors, even if pictured, are not always included.

Shipping

We prefer to use the Fedex Hold-At-Location service, meaning we ship your watch to the Fedex Office or Ship Center nearest you, where you can pick it up upon arrival. All of our shipments are insured and shipped Priority Overnight.

International shipping to select countries is done via Fedex International Priority shipping. Please be aware of all appropriate customs and import taxes you may incur. Failure to accept an international package will result in restocking fees on a case-by-case basis. We will ship to the nearest Fedex Ship Center to you, and you can pickup the package at your convenience.

In Person Showings

For safety reasons, we do not encourage in person showings or browsing. All of our operations are online and we deliver via Fedex Priority Overnight.

Trades

We happily take trades of Rolex, Omega, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe and most major brands towards any of our listed watches. If you wish to trade in your watch, please send photos and relevant information in your initial message, along with your desired trade value. Our offer for your watch is not binding and is subject to change or revocation. We process all of our trades via insured Fedex Priority Overnight shipping; we ship the watch you are receiving after we receive your watch and the appropriate cleared payment, if a balance is due to us.

Price Suggestions

We may have Price Suggestions enabled on some of our listings; this does not mean we are obligated to negotiate on these listings nor provide price reductions.

Payment

Payment is accepted through Chrono24 Trusted Checkout. We cannot hold watches for longer than 24 hours without proof of payment / payment confirmation. Please submit payment for your watch as soon as your order is confirmed to avoid delays and order cancellation.

All purchases are subject to the sales tax rate of your shipping address.

Returns

We offer a 100% Money Back Guarantee on all of our watches! Feel free to shop with confidence. You have 14 days to return your item. Watches must be in the exact same condition as they were shipped, not sized or altered in any way. If watches are not returned in the same condition, restocking fees may apply.

*We are not an authorized dealer for any of the brands that we list (as are all resellers), however all of our watches are guaranteed authentic.*

Warranty

Since we are not an Official Rolex Authorized Dealer, we’re unable to make claims regarding the official Rolex manufacturer’s warranty.

However, every watch we sell is backed by our own One-Year Warranty for your peace of mind. Each timepiece includes a one-year warranty card that covers movement-related defects affecting the proper mechanical function of the watch. This includes issues such as the watch stopping unexpectedly, running significantly fast or slow, or the power reserve not functioning as intended.

All warranty claims are evaluated on a case-by-case basis by our authorized technicians, and coverage is determined after inspection to confirm that the issue is due to a movement defect rather than external damage, normal wear and tear, or misuse.

Intense white, offering excellent contrast and clean modern appeal. ￼
• Markers: Applied baton (stick) hour indices, often with luminescent material for low‑light visibility. ￼
• Hands: Polished gold‑tone hands that stand out sharply against the white background and typically also have luminescent fill for nighttime legibility. ￼
• 24‑Hour Display: Includes an off‑centre 24‑hour disc that shows a second time zone — a signature feature of the Sky‑Dweller. ￼
• Date & Annual Calendar: A date window at 3 o’clock with a Cyclops magnifying lens sits within the dial, and the annual calendar function uses small apertures beside the hour markers to indicate the month. ￼
• Legibility: The bright white dial enhances contrast with the gold hands and markers, making time easy to read at a glance in most lighting conditions. ￼

🪶 Other Notable Design Elements
• Bezel: Fluted yellow‑gold Ring Command bezel — iconic Rolex design that also interfaces with the watch’s functions. ￼
• Bracelet: Typically paired with a two‑tone Jubilee or Oyster bracelet in Oystersteel and 18 k yellow gold. ￼

🛠️ Functionality

This reference is powered by Rolex’s automatic Calibre 9002 movement, offering:
• Dual time zone display
• An annual calendar that only needs adjustment once per year (in February)
• Approximately 72‑hour power reserve
• Water resistance ~100 m (330 ft) ￼

In essence: the white dial of this Sky‑Dweller presents a crisp, luxurious, and highly functional face — combining clarity with Rolex’s signature technical sophistication and design language. ￼

column83:
The stainless steel 36 x 43.5 x 11.5 mm case appears to have been lightly polished in the past, remaining very thick, even, crisp, and clean, exhibiting only minor surface wear, as photographed. The reference 16234 and “E” serial are clearly visible between the lugs.

Dial: The silver soleil or sunburst dial has aged and tropicalized to a warm rose color that is very even and attractive across the dial. The tritium lume on the dial and hands emits a uniform UV signature that drops off quickly, yet has retained a more eggshell appearance. There is one minor surface mark to the tropicalization (it doesn’t appear to impact the dial) between the 6 and 7 o’clock indices, that was not even noticeable until the watch was photographed. This is not readily apparent on wrist, and appears exaggerated in the macro photos provided.

Movement: The caliber 3135 automatic winding, hacking, and quickset date movement is winding, setting, changing date, and running smoothly at approximately -6 s/d, averaged across 6 positions. On wrist for a day, I found timekeeping to be within 3 seconds. Service history is unknown, though with any watch, Rolex especially, I always recommend servicing as needed rather than proactively introducing the possibility for issues.

Bracelet: The 62510 H jubilee bracelet is in very nice condition throughout with a date matching “S 2” bracelet clasp code indicating production in the 2nd month of 1994. The bracelet has 18 links, containing 3 screws, meaning 1 link is removable and will fit up to a 7.25” wrist.

Includes: Inner leather box with pillow and sueded “envelope”, original purchase receipt, Oyster hang tag, COSC hangtag, Oyster booklet, Datejust booklet, Translation booklet, calendar, and leather document holder.

Notes

This Rolex features a 31mm steel case that sits in overall good unpolished condition. Its white/silvered dial has noticeable patina to its indices and outer dial edge, specifically pertaining to what looks the be the sealant material to the indices. Its steel hands and blue anodized seconds hand both show visible wear upon closer inspection. The inside of the case back shows a “6431” stamp thought the watch is stamped 6430 between its lugs. This most likely the result of a service intervention, either by Rolex or an authorized watchmaker. During routine maintenance or repairs, case backs from similar or contemporary models could be used if the original was damaged or missing. Since references 6430 and 6431 share nearly identical specifications and case dimensions, their parts are functionally interchangeable. This piece is powered by a Cal. 1215 manual-wind movement and is fitted with a dark brown genuine calf skin strap. Overall, this Rolex sits in fair vintage condition, showing wear consistent with its age.

column853:
36 mm round Oyster case (Diameter ~36 mm), monobloc middle case, screw‑down case‑back and screwed crown (Twinlock) — ensuring water resistance and robustness. ￼
• Bezel: Fluted yellow‑gold bezel — a hallmark of many Datejust models; with a motif dial, the fluted-bezel design influences not just the bezel, but also the dial’s texture in some variants, creating a subtle mirrored effect. ￼
• Crystal & Date: Scratch‑resistant sapphire crystal, and a date window at 3 o’clock with Rolex’s signature Cyclops magnifier for easier date reading. ￼
• Bracelet: Typically fitted with a Jubilee bracelet (five‑piece links) in two-tone steel + yellow gold — combining comfort, classic looks, and luxury. ￼
• Water resistance: 100 meters (330 feet) — practical for everyday wear (rain, splashes, daily exposure), though it remains more a glamour/classic watch than a heavy-duty diver. ￼

🌀 Dial: What “Motif Dial” Means & How It Looks
• Patterned / Textured Dial — Unlike a plain flat or sunburst dial, the “motif” dial features a carefully applied textured motif. For example, the “fluted motif” dial echoes the ridged design of a Datejust’s fluted bezel, but on the surface of the dial itself — tiny patterned lines or ridges radiating/arranged to catch light at different angles. ￼
• Variants: The motif dial appears in different motif styles depending on specific 126233. Some are “fluted motif,” others are “palm‑motif” (a more tropical / patterned aesthetic), and there are also options possibly with diamond indices depending on the variant. ￼
• Visual Impact: The patterned dial gives extra depth and sophistication: under different lighting and wrist angles, the texture shifts subtly, giving a sense of dynamic surface rather than a static dial — this makes the watch stand out compared to a standard plain-dial Datejust. ￼
• Hour markers / Hands: Depending on the specific motif variant, markers may be plain batons (or applied indices), often in yellow‑gold tone to match the bezel/bracelet elements. The result is a refined two-tone aesthetic — pattern + precious metal accents. ￼

⏱️ Movement & Performance
• Inside the 126233 beats Rolex’s in‑house Calibre 3235 — a modern automatic (self‑winding) movement. ￼
• Power reserve: approximately 70 hours when fully wound — generous autonomy for a daily-wear watch. ￼
• Accuracy & Certification: The watch is a “Superlative Chronometer” after casing — meaning Rolex internally certifies ±2 seconds/day (post‑casing), giving reliable daily precision. ￼
• Functions: Hours, minutes, seconds + date (with instantaneous date change, quick‑set, Cyclops magnifier) — keeping the classic Datejust simplicity and practicality. ￼

🎯 Style, Character & Who It’s For

The 126233 with a motif dial is ideal if you want a watch that combines:
• Classic Datejust Heritage + Unique Aesthetic — you get the timeless Rolex Datejust DNA (case shape, fluted bezel, Jubilee bracelet, robust movement) with a dial that adds personality and modern flair (pattern, texture, champagne/gold tones or motif variants).
• Everyday Wearability + Dressy Elegance — 36 mm case size works well on many wrists; two‑tone steel + yellow gold + patterned dial gives a classy look that transitions from daily wear to smart outfits or evening attire.
• Subtle Sophistication — the motif dial is noticeable but tasteful; under light, its texture catches the eye without being loud. Good if you like detail and refinement more than flashy “bling.”
• Legacy + Modern Performance — with calibre 3235 and Rolex’s construction quality, you get a watch built for long-term wear, reliability, and classic styling — with a dial that still feels contemporary.

👍 What Makes the Motif‑Dial 126233 Special / Pros & What to Know

Pros
• Dial texture adds visual depth and uniqueness — stands out among standard dials without being over the top.
• Two‑tone Rolesor and fluted bezel give the classic “luxury Rolex” vibe but remain versatile for many outfits.
• Modern movement with long power reserve and date functionality — practical for everyday use.
• 36 mm case remains comfortably wearable and elegant on many wrist sizes

column943:
The case measures 40 mm in diameter with a thick, robust profile typical of the “super case” generation. It has broad lugs and crown guards, giving it a strong wrist presence. The case is crafted entirely from Rolex’s proprietary 18k yellow gold.

Bezel:
It features a unidirectional rotating bezel with a blue Cerachrom (ceramic) insert. The bezel is used to measure elapsed dive time and is highly scratch resistant and fade resistant. The numerals and graduations are engraved and filled with gold.

Dial:
The dial is a sunburst blue that shifts in tone depending on lighting. It has large applied gold hour markers filled with luminous material for visibility in low light. The hands are the classic Mercedes style, also in yellow gold with luminous fill. A date window appears at 3 o’clock with the Cyclops magnification lens on the sapphire crystal.

Movement:
Inside is Rolex’s in-house Caliber 3135 automatic movement. It is a chronometer-certified movement known for reliability and precision. It offers approximately 48 hours of power reserve and includes a quickset date function.

Bracelet and clasp:
The watch comes on a solid 18k yellow gold Oyster bracelet. It features an Oysterlock safety clasp and Glidelock extension system, allowing fine adjustment of the bracelet length without tools.

Water resistance:
It is water resistant to 300 meters (1,000 feet), consistent with the Submariner’s professional dive watch heritage.

Overall character:
The 116618LB combines the functionality of a professional dive watch with the weight and visual impact of solid gold. It is bold, highly noticeable, and carries significant wrist presence. column1115:
116515LN
Condition: Excellent!
Case Size: 40mm
Material: "Everose" (rose gold), meteorite, Cerachrom

Description:
The 116515LN meteorite Daytona is a true showstopper and statement piece, a watch to crown a collection. This stunning iteration of the Daytona features an Everose case and clasp (Rolex's proprietary blended rose gold alloy) and the supremely comfortable Oysterflex rubber strap. The dial is the real talking point of the 116515, featuring a slice of meteorite straight from the Gibeon meteorite in Namibia, Africa. The material itself makes this model rarer and more collectable, but so does the tightening of Namibia's laws. Rolex purchased a sizable amount of meteorite from the Gibeon meteorite, but in 2004, the state introduced the National Heritage Act, which forbade the exporting of meteorite from Namibia without the state's consent. This means that Rolex has a backstock of now-protected meteorite used for certain dials. This means each Rolex with a meteorite dial gets more and more rare. Powering the 116515 is Rolex's in-house Caliber 4130 automatic movement with 72 hours of power reserve.

Contents of the set:
This watch comes as a full set with box and papers! Strap size is D & F.

Where we are located:
Charlotte, NC

About us:
We strive to provide a smooth and positive experience for anyone looking to purchase or sell a timepiece. We serve clients across the world. We have hundreds of references across all platforms & forums. We look forward to working with you on your next timepiece!

A rare, elegant classic, 14k gold and stainless steel Rolex Lady Oyster bubble back automatic, time tested luxury, ready to be worn and enjoyed, make a statement with this classic classic luxury! Experience timeless elegance with the Rolex 79160 Oyster Perpetual Date, a refined 26mm timepiece that seamlessly combines form and function. Rolex constructed this model entirely from stainless steel, delivering both lasting durability and a clean, modern aesthetic in a size that’s perfect for everyday wear.

To enhance its sleek design, Rolex equipped the watch with a smooth bezel that frames a crisp white dial. The dial features Arabic numerals and luminescent hour markers, providing excellent readability in any lighting. Additionally, the date display at 3 o’clock, magnified by the signature Cyclops lens, offers practical functionality without disrupting the dial’s elegant layout.

Moreover, a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal protects the dial, while the automatic movement ensures reliable timekeeping. Finally, the Oyster bracelet offers a secure and comfortable fit for wrists up to 6 inches, and the fold-over clasp adds an extra layer of confidence for everyday wear.

Brushed & polished stainless steel case. Polished stainless steel Triplock screw-down crown. Domed sapphire crystal with a thickness of 5.5mm. Rolex Ringlock System case architecture. Helium escape valve on the left case edge. Bezel with polished black Cerachrom ceramic bezel insert, featuring matte platinum coated 60 minute scale & a luminous dot at zero. D-Blue graduating blue to black dial. Polished hands with luminous fill. Applied polished hour markers with luminous fill. Fine printed minutes/seconds track. Date window at 3 o'clock.

Bracelet: Fitted on its original brushed stainless steel Oyster bracelet with a Folding Oysterlock safety clasp & with a Glidelock extension system, which allows for quick sizing adjustments in 2mm increments without the need of tools, for a total of 20mm. The bracelet is also fitted with a Fliplock extension link for fitting over a diving suit. The bracelet is fully linked.

Movement: Powered by a Rolex in-house calibre 3235, beats at 28,800 vph (4Hz), contains 31 jewels & has a power reserve of +/- 70 hours. Features Rolex's Paramagnetic blue Parachrom hairspring & a bi-directional self-winding rotor.

Condition: In excellent near-new condition. We have had the watch lightly polished to remove most signs of wear. The watch beats strong & keeps excellent accurate time. We would be comfortable rating this watch in a 97%+ overall condition. Delivered in its original box with the all papers, and with both the green hologram & white serialized hang tags.

column570:
A rare and distinguished Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date 36 reference 19018 crafted in solid 18K yellow gold, produced in 1986. This model embodies a unique chapter in Rolex history, featuring a precise and reliable quartz movement developed entirely in-house by Rolex. The champagne dial is complemented by pinball hour markers and a traditional fluted bezel. This watch is all original.

Dial:

The watch features a factory champagne dial with pinball hour markers, surrounded by Roman numeral minute markers on the outer track. The iconic Rolex crown sits at 12 o’clock above the day window, while the date window at 3 o’clock is magnified by a sapphire crystal with Cyclops lens. The hands are matching gold-tone.

Case:

The 18K yellow gold case measures 36mm in diameter and is fitted with a fluted bezel and sapphire crystal. The crown screws down securely, and the watch is rated water resistant to 10 ATM.

Bracelet:

The watch is fitted with the original integrated solid 18K yellow gold Oysterquartz style bracelet. The bracelet has a polished and brushed finish and is secured by a hidden fold-over clasp. It fits wrists up to approximately 7.15 inch wrist.

Condition:

This Rolex Oysterquartz Day-Date is in excellent vintage condition, showing only minor surface scratches on the case, case back and bracelet. The movement has been machine-tested for accuracy and water resistance, and performs within Rolex standards.

Product Description:

This Oyster Perpetual Explorer II in Oystersteel with an Oyster bracelet features a white dial with an arrow-shaped 24-hour hand and hour markers with a Chromalight display.
Reliability in the toughest conditions
The Explorer II is equipped with an additional 24-hour display; a dedicated hand circles the dial in 24 hours rather than the usual 12, pointing to a fixed bezel with 24 hour graduations.
This function enables the wearer to distinguish the hours of the day from the hours of the night, an essential feature for those who venture into environments where darkness reigns supreme – such as in the depths of caves, or where the sun never sets – like the polar regions in summer.
Distinguishing day from night
The Explorer II features a date display, an additional orange 24-hour hand and a fixed bezel with 24-hour graduations, enabling day to be distinguished from night.
It became the watch of choice for speleologists, volcanologists and polar explorers. Its hour markers and hands feature a highly-legible Chromalight display with long-lasting blue luminescence.
Highly resistant to corrosion
Rolex uses Oystersteel for its steel watch cases. Specially developed by the brand, Oystersteel belongs to the 904L steel family, alloys most commonly used in high-technology and in the aerospace and chemical industries, where maximum resistance to corrosion is essential.
Oystersteel is extremely resistant, offers an exceptional finish once polished and maintains its beauty even in the harshest environments.
Alchemy of form and function
The Oyster bracelet is a perfect alchemy of form and function, aesthetics and technology, designed to be both robust and comfortable. It is equipped with an Oysterlock folding clasp, which prevents accidental opening and the Easylink comfort extension link, also exclusive to Rolex.
This ingenious system allows the wearer to increase the bracelet length by approximately 5 mm, providing additional comfort in any circumstance.

Polished stainless steel case & Twinlock screw-down crown. Polished 18kt white gold fluted bezel. Black dial. Polished hands. Partially skeletonized minute & hour hands so not to obscure the dual time. Hour & minute hands with luminous filled tips. Applied white gold index hour markers with luminous fill. Printed minute/seconds track on the dial perimeter. Features: RING COMMAND BEZEL - Watch setting & Corrections: The bezel is key to setting the 3 functions of the watch (time, dual-time & annual calendar). The "RING COMMAND BEZEL" features 3 positions, each position corresponds to a different function of the watch. Setting the bezel to the position of the corresponding watch function will allow the correction of that particular function via the crown.
GMT/Dual Time-Zone:
Off-center silvered 24 hour ring.
Red triangle indicating the additional time-zone.
Indicated in 24 hour mode to easily distinguish between AM & PM at the additional time-zone location.
The dual time-zone is adjustable in one hour increments & can be adjusted without stopping or interrupting the operation of the movement.
SAROS Annual Calendar:
Date window at 3 o'clock with a "cyclops" magnifier.
Month display via small windows at each hour marker (12 hours = 12 month). A red filled display window indicates the current month.
A manual correction is required only once a year on March 1st to compensate for the shorter 28 or 29 days of February.
The date can be adjusted both forward & backward.
Bracelet: Fitted on its original brushed & polished stainless steel Jubilee style bracelet with a Rolex Folding Oysterclasp featuring an Easylink 5mm comfort extension link. The bracelet has all links & will fit up to a 8" wrist.

Movement: Powered by a Rolex in-house calibre 9001 which beats at 28,800 vph, contains 40 jewels & has an approximate power reserve of 72 hours. The movement features a Paramagnetic blue Parachrom hairspring & high performance Paraflex shock absorbers. The watch boasts a total of 14 patents, seven of which are on the movement.

Condition: In excellent near-new condition. We have had the watch lightly polished to remove most signs of wear. We would be comfortable rating this watch in a 97%+ overall condition. Delivered in its original box with the all its original papers, with the Rolex CPO service warranty card & with both the green hologram & white hang tags.

Product Description:

The Oyster Perpetual 41 with a bright blue dial and an Oyster bracelet.
A watchmaking technique
The sunray finish creates delicate light reflections on many dials in the Oyster Perpetual collection. It is obtained using masterful brushing techniques that create grooves running outwards from the centre of the dial.
Light is diffused consistently along each engraving, creating a characteristic subtle glow that moves depending on the position of the wrist. Once the sunray finish has been completed, the dial colour is applied using Physical Vapour Deposition or electroplating. A light coat of varnish gives the dial its final look.
Highly resistant to corrosion
Rolex uses Oystersteel for its steel watch cases. Specially developed by the brand, Oystersteel belongs to the 904L steel family, alloys most commonly used in high-technology and in the aerospace and chemical industries, where maximum resistance to corrosion is essential.
Oystersteel is extremely resistant, offers an exceptional finish once polished and maintains its beauty even in the harshest environments.
Alchemy of form and function
The Oyster bracelet is a perfect alchemy of form and function, aesthetics and technology.
First introduced in the late 1930s, this particularly robust and comfortable metal bracelet with its broad, flat three-piece links remains the most universal bracelet in the Oyster collection. For the Oyster Perpetual models the Oyster bracelet is fitted with an Oysterclasp.
